LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PINNACLE INTERNATIONAL DEVELOPMENT INC.'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE AND FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTIONS 395.5, 396B AND 397.THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT PINNACLE INTERNATIONAL'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE IS DENIED. ON A MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE, THE BURDEN IS ON THE MOVING PARTY TO SHOW (1) THAT VENUE IS PROPER IN A DIFFERENT COUNTY THAN THE ONE IN WHICH THE LAWSUIT WAS FILED AND (2) THAT VENUE IS IMPROPER IN THE CURRENT COUNTY UNDER ANY APPLICABLE THEORY. (LA MIRADA COMM. HOSP. V. SUP. CT. (1967) 249 CAL.APP.2D 39, 42.) DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT VENUE IS IMPROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY. BOTH PARTIES' REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-28-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2020 Continued To Oct-14-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 397
Off Calendar Jul-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Helene A. Simvoulakis-Panos (408-918-2809; hsimvoulakis@pahl-mccay.com) for Plaintiff Flexible Funding Ltd. Liability Co.
Jamie Altman Buggy (858.779.1722; jbuggy@cgs3.com) of Crosbie Gliner Schiffman Southard & Swanson LLP for defendant Pinnacle International Development

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PINNACLE INTERNATIONAL DEVELOPMENT INC.'s Motion To Change Venue Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 397:

Defendant Pinnacle International's motion to change venue to San Diego is granted. Defendant shall pay all required transfer fees. In the San Diego action, Constructure, Inc. seeks approximately $3.6 million arising out of subcontracts entered into in San Diego, and mechanic's liens recorded in San Diego, relating to a residential construction project located in San Diego. (Pinnacle's Ex. 7.) That sum ostensibly includes the $526,414 in Technical Works invoices which is at issue in this case. Given this overlap, the dispute should be resolved in one forum. In the interests of justice and judicial economy, the court transfers this action to San Diego.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-22-2020 Continued To Aug-12-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2020 Continued To Jun-10-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue And For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Counsel Pursuant To C.C.P. Sections 395.5, 396b And 397
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Catherine Robertson, Esq. 408-918-2835 of Pahl & McCay for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Jaime Altman Buggy, Esq. 858-779-1722 CGS# LLP for Defendant Pinnacle International.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues and requests to deny without prejudice. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PINNACLE INTERNATIONAL DEVELOPMENT INC.'s Motion To Change Venue And For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Counsel Pursuant To C.C.P. Sections 395.5, 396b And 397.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Pinnacle International's motion to change venue is denied. On a motion to change venue, the burden is on the moving party to show (1) that venue is proper in a different county than the one in which the lawsuit was filed and (2) that venue is improper in the current county under any applicable theory. (La Mirada Comm. Hosp. v. Sup. Ct. (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 39, 42.) Defendant fails to show that venue is improper in San Francisco County. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. (see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
